mnemonics.title: Normal Form Progression
mnemonics.content:
1NF: No Repeats (atomic values)
2NF: No Partial (full key dependency)
3NF: No Transit (key-only dependency)
BCNF: Key Determinant (all FDs from keys)

mnemonics.title: Anomaly Types
mnemonics.content:
INSERT Can't Add (missing info)
DELETE Lose Data (unintended loss)
UPDATE Inconsistent (redundant updates)

mnemonics.title: Dependency Check
mnemonics.content:
Partial: Non-key → Part of Key
Transitive: Non-key → Non-key → Key
Check order: 1NF→2NF→3NF→BCNF

acronyms.usage: All columns contain indivisible values, no repeating groups
No partial dependencies on partial key
No transitive dependencies from primary key
Every determinant is a candidate key
X→Y means X determines Y uniquely
Minimal superkey, one becomes primary key
Chosen candidate key to identify records uniquely

visualTips.visualization: Draw a table with single-valued cells. Show incorrect: cells with lists/arrays, correct: each cell has one value. Use grid with one value per cell.
For composite key (A,B)→C, show dependency diagram: A→D is wrong (arrow from part of key to non-key). Draw key as two connected boxes, non-key as separate box.
Show transitive chain: Key→X→Y. Draw three boxes in chain. Breaking the chain means moving Y to new table with X as key.
Show all FDs must originate from candidate keys. Draw all candidate keys, ensure every arrow starts from a candidate key box.

analogies.analogy: Like organizing a closet: no boxes containing multiple items. Each shelf has one category of items, not mixed.
Like a team project: everyone contributes to the final deliverable, not just a part. Partial work (partial dependency) is not allowed.
Like a reporting chain: employees report to manager, manager reports to CEO. No employee skipping levels (transitive).
Like a constitution: all laws must originate from the elected representatives (the people/keys), not from unelected bodies.
Like organizing a library: fiction, non-fiction, reference sections. Related items together, no mixing, easy to find.
Like splitting a large document into chapters. Each chapter stands alone but together tells the full story.
